scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant is known to the Plaintiff and had approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance by way of a hand loan for his personal requirements. Acting on the request and based on trust, the Plaintiff agreed to advance a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant.

6. Pursuant to the agreement, the Plaintiff transferred the said sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant's bank account on 15.03.2024 via NEFT. The transaction is evidenced by the bank transfer record bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, which is marked as **Annexure A**.

7. Although there was no formal written loan agreement executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages exchanged on {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATE}}. The copies of the said digital communications are marked as **Annexure B**.

8. It was orally agreed between the parties that the Defendant shall repay the principal amount along with interest at the rate of 12% per annum. The Defendant was obligated to repay the amount on demand or within a reasonable time, failing which interest would accrue.

BREACH AND DEFAULT

9. The Defendant failed to repay the principal amount as per the oral understanding. The Defendant committed default in repayment, and accordingly, the date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is taken as 20.06.2024.

10. Despite several oral requests and reminders made to the Defendant to return the loan amount, the Defendant postponed the repayment on one pretext or another and failed to discharge the liability.

11. The Plaintiff's counsel issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues, which is marked as **Annexure C**. The Defendant {{LEGAL_NOTICE_REPLY_STATUS}} to the said notice and has failed to pay the dues, thereby committing a breach of the obligation to repay.

DAMAGES

12. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- which was advanced as a loan and remains outstanding to date.

13. The Defendant is further liable to pay interest on the said principal amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation. The interest accrued up to the date of filing of the plaint amounts to Rs. {{CALCULATED_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}.

14. The total amount recoverable from the Defendant comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- and the accrued interest of Rs. {{CALCULATED_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}/-, along with future interest and costs of the suit. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree for the recovery of the said amounts.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

16. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

18. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

19. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of ac
